recipes.title: Mapo Tofu
recipes.description: Sichuan classic of soft tofu in a fiery sauce of doubanjiang and Sichuan peppercorn with minced beef.
steps: Cube tofu and blanch in salted water for 2 minutes, then drain.
Toast Sichuan peppercorns and grind to powder.
Heat oil in a wok and brown beef until crisp.
Add doubanjiang, douchi, garlic and ginger and stir until oil turns red.
Pour in stock and add tofu, simmering gently for 5 minutes.
Thicken with cornstarch slurry, finish with peppercorn powder and scallions.

recipes.title: Kung Pao Chicken
recipes.description: Stir-fried chicken with peanuts, dried chillies and Sichuan peppercorns in a sweet-tart sauce.
steps: Cube chicken and toss with 1 teaspoon cornstarch and 1 tablespoon soy sauce.
Mix remaining soy sauce, vinegar, sugar and cornstarch with 3 tablespoons water for the sauce.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat and stir-fry chicken until just cooked.
Push chicken aside, add chillies and peppercorns and toast 30 seconds.
Add garlic, ginger and scallion whites and stir 30 seconds.
Pour in sauce, toss until thickened, then fold in peanuts and scallion greens.

recipes.title: Xiaolongbao
recipes.description: Shanghai soup dumplings with pork filling and a burst of rich aspic broth inside.
steps: Mix flour with hot water to form a dough, knead 10 minutes and rest 30 minutes.
Combine pork with chopped aspic, soy sauce, wine, grated ginger, sesame oil and sugar.
Roll dough into a log, cut into small pieces and roll into thin discs with thicker centres.
Place a tablespoon of filling on each disc and pleat 18 times around the edge.
Steam over high heat for 8 minutes on parchment in a bamboo basket.
Serve with black vinegar and julienned ginger.

recipes.title: Peking Duck
recipes.description: Roast duck with shatteringly crisp skin, served with thin pancakes, scallions and hoisin.
steps: Loosen the skin of the duck from the meat by inflating with a straw.
Pour boiling water over the duck to tighten skin, repeat three times.
Brush duck with maltose mixed with vinegar, hang to dry overnight in a cool place.
Rub cavity with five spice and salt, then roast at 200C for 90 minutes.
Carve crisp skin and meat into thin slices.
Serve in pancakes with scallion strips, cucumber sticks and hoisin sauce.

recipes.title: Char Siu
recipes.description: Cantonese sweet and savoury barbecued pork with a glossy red glaze.
steps: Slice pork into long strips about 4 cm thick.
Mix hoisin, soy sauce, half the honey, wine, five spice, crushed garlic, sugar and red yeast powder.
Marinate pork strips for at least 8 hours in the fridge.
Roast on a rack at 200C for 25 minutes, basting with marinade twice.
Brush with maltose mixed with remaining honey and roast 5 minutes more until lacquered.
Rest 10 minutes and slice.

recipes.title: Wonton Soup
recipes.description: Cantonese pork and shrimp wontons in a clear, fragrant chicken broth.
steps: Chop shrimp roughly and combine with pork, soy sauce, sesame oil, white pepper, grated ginger and scallions.
Place a teaspoon of filling on each wrapper, wet edges and fold into bonnets.
Bring chicken stock to a boil and season lightly.
Drop wontons into a separate pot of boiling water and cook 4 minutes until they float.
Blanch bok choy in stock for 1 minute.
Divide wontons and bok choy into bowls and ladle hot stock over them.

recipes.title: Hot and Sour Soup
recipes.description: Tangy and peppery soup with tofu, mushrooms, bamboo shoots and egg ribbons.
steps: Soak wood ears 20 minutes, then slice with the shiitake, bamboo shoots and tofu into julienne.
Bring stock to a boil, add mushrooms, bamboo shoots and tofu and simmer 5 minutes.
Stir in soy sauce, vinegar and white pepper.
Mix cornstarch with 4 tablespoons water and stir into soup until thickened.
Pour in beaten eggs in a slow stream while stirring to form ribbons.
Finish with sesame oil and scallions.

recipes.title: Yangzhou Fried Rice
recipes.description: Classic egg fried rice with char siu, shrimp, peas and carrots.
steps: Use day-old chilled rice, breaking up clumps with your hands.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a wok and quickly cook beaten eggs into curds, then remove.
Add remaining oil, stir-fry shrimp and diced char siu briefly.
Add diced carrot and peas and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add rice and toss for 3 minutes over high heat.
Return eggs, drizzle soy sauce, salt and white pepper, and finish with scallions.

recipes.title: Sweet and Sour Pork
recipes.description: Crispy battered pork tossed in a glossy sweet and sour sauce with bell peppers and pineapple.
steps: Cube pork and toss with beaten egg and a pinch of salt.
Coat each piece thickly in cornstarch.
Heat oil to 175C and fry pork in batches until golden, then drain.
Reheat oil to 190C and double-fry pork for 30 seconds for extra crisp.
In a wok, simmer ketchup, vinegar, sugar and soy sauce until syrupy.
Toss in peppers, onion and pineapple, then return pork and coat with sauce.

recipes.title: Mooncake
recipes.description: Traditional Mid-Autumn pastry with golden syrup crust and lotus seed paste filling.
steps: Whisk golden syrup, peanut oil and alkaline water, then sift in flour to form a soft dough. Rest 1 hour.
Wrap each salted egg yolk in 60 g of lotus paste to form a ball.
Divide dough into 30 g pieces and flatten each into a disc.
Wrap dough around a paste ball, sealing completely.
Press into a mooncake mould to imprint a pattern.
Bake at 180C for 5 minutes, brush with egg wash, then bake 12 minutes more until golden.

recipes.title: Zongzi
recipes.description: Sticky rice dumplings wrapped in bamboo leaves, traditionally eaten at Dragon Boat Festival.
steps: Soak rice overnight, drain and toss with soy sauces and five spice.
Soak bamboo leaves in warm water for 2 hours, then wash thoroughly.
Marinate pork in soy sauce for 2 hours and rehydrate mushrooms.
Fold two leaves into a cone, fill with rice, add pork, mushroom and yolk, top with rice.
Fold leaves over the top and tie firmly with twine into a tetrahedron.
Boil parcels in plenty of water for 3 hours, weighted to keep submerged.

recipes.title: Dan Dan Noodles
recipes.description: Sichuan noodles in a chilli oil sauce with ground pork, preserved vegetable and peppercorn.
steps: Toast Sichuan peppercorns and grind to powder.
Stir-fry pork in oil with ya cai until crisp, season with 1 tablespoon soy sauce.
In each bowl mix chilli oil, sesame paste, remaining soy sauce, vinegar, grated garlic and peppercorn powder.
Cook noodles until just tender and drain.
Pile noodles on top of sauce, top with crispy pork and scallions.
Stir thoroughly before eating.

recipes.title: Beef and Broccoli
recipes.description: Velvety stir-fried beef with broccoli florets in a savoury oyster sauce gravy.
steps: Slice beef thinly against the grain and toss with 1 tablespoon soy sauce, wine and 1 tablespoon cornstarch.
Blanch broccoli for 90 seconds in salted water and drain.
Stir remaining cornstarch with stock, oyster sauce, remaining soy sauce and sugar.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a wok and sear beef in a single layer for 1 minute, then remove.
Add remaining oil with garlic and ginger, then return broccoli and beef.
Pour in sauce and toss until glossy and thick.

recipes.title: Dumplings
recipes.description: Northern-style boiled jiaozi with pork and chive filling, dipped in vinegar and chilli oil.
steps: Salt the chopped cabbage, rest 10 minutes, then squeeze out excess water.
Mix pork with chopped chives, cabbage, grated ginger, soy sauce, wine, sesame oil, salt and pepper.
Place a tablespoon of filling on each wrapper, wet edges and pleat into half-moons.
Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil.
Drop dumplings in batches and cook until they float, then add cold water and cook 2 more minutes.
Serve with black vinegar dipping sauce.

recipes.title: Twice Cooked Pork
recipes.description: Sichuan favourite of pork belly first boiled, then stir-fried with leeks and doubanjiang.
steps: Boil pork belly with ginger and a splash of wine for 25 minutes, then cool.
Slice the cooked pork into thin pieces.
Heat oil in a wok and render pork slices until edges curl.
Add doubanjiang, douchi and minced garlic and stir until fragrant.
Stir in sweet bean sauce, soy sauce and sugar.
Toss in sliced leeks and stir-fry 1 minute until just tender.

recipes.title: Hot Pot
recipes.description: Communal Chinese hot pot with thinly sliced meats, tofu and vegetables cooked in spiced broth.
steps: Simmer beef bones for 3 hours to make a stock, then strain.
In a hot pot vessel, fry doubanjiang, chillies, peppercorns and star anise in oil.
Add stock and bring to a simmer to make the spicy broth.
Set up the hot pot at the table over a burner.
Diners cook meats, tofu, mushrooms, cabbage and noodles to taste.
Serve with sesame paste dipping sauce mixed with garlic, soy and chilli oil.

recipes.title: Chow Mein
recipes.description: Stir-fried egg noodles with chicken, bean sprouts, scallions and soy sauce.
steps: Boil noodles for 3 minutes, drain and toss with 1 teaspoon oil.
Slice chicken thinly and toss with 1 tablespoon soy sauce.
Heat oil in a wok and stir-fry chicken until cooked through, then remove.
Stir-fry julienned carrot and garlic for 1 minute, add bean sprouts and scallions.
Return chicken with noodles, dark soy sauce, remaining soy sauce and oyster sauce.
Toss over high heat for 2 minutes, finish with sesame oil.

recipes.title: Congee
recipes.description: Slow-cooked rice porridge served with century egg, pork floss and scallions.
steps: Rinse rice and combine with water, stock and sliced ginger.
Bring to a boil, then simmer with the lid ajar, stirring often.
Cook 90 minutes until rice is broken down and silky.
Season with salt and white pepper.
Ladle into bowls and top with chopped century egg, pork floss and scallions.
Drizzle with sesame oil before serving.

recipes.title: Salt and Pepper Shrimp
recipes.description: Cantonese shrimp deep-fried in their shells and tossed with toasted salt, pepper and chillies.
steps: Trim shrimp antennae and devein through the shell.
Toast salt, white pepper and Sichuan peppercorns, then grind to powder.
Toss shrimp with cornstarch.
Heat oil to 190C and fry shrimp 90 seconds until shells are crisp, then drain.
In a clean wok, stir-fry minced garlic, sliced chillies and scallion whites in 1 tablespoon oil.
Return shrimp, sprinkle salt-pepper mix and toss to coat.

recipes.title: Stir-fried Water Spinach
recipes.description: Quick stir-fry of water spinach with garlic, chilli and fermented bean curd.
steps: Wash water spinach and cut into 6 cm lengths.
Mash fermented bean curd with soy sauce and sugar.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat and stir-fry garlic and chillies for 20 seconds.
Add water spinach and toss continuously for 90 seconds.
Add fermented bean curd mixture and salt and stir for another minute.
Serve immediately while bright green.

recipes.title: Fish-Fragrant Eggplant
recipes.description: Sichuan stir-fried eggplant in a savoury, garlicky chilli sauce called yuxiang.
steps: Cut eggplants into batons, salt for 15 minutes, then pat dry.
Mix soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, cornstarch and stock for the sauce.
Heat oil in a wok and shallow fry eggplants until soft, then drain.
In remaining oil stir-fry pork until crisp, add doubanjiang, ginger and garlic.
Return eggplant and pour in sauce, tossing until thickened.
Finish with scallion greens.

recipes.title: Lion's Head Meatballs
recipes.description: Large, tender Shanghai-style pork meatballs braised with napa cabbage in a light gravy.
steps: Mix pork with egg, cornstarch, soy sauce, wine, grated ginger, chopped scallions and salt.
Stir vigorously in one direction until sticky, then form into 4 large balls.
Heat oil in a pot and brown each meatball on all sides.
Line the bottom of the pot with cabbage leaves, place meatballs on top.
Pour in stock, cover and simmer 45 minutes.
Add cabbage hearts in the last 10 minutes and serve.

recipes.title: Egg Drop Soup
recipes.description: Simple Chinese soup of chicken stock thickened with cornstarch and silky egg ribbons.
steps: Bring chicken stock to a simmer with grated ginger.
Mix cornstarch with 4 tablespoons cold water and stir into stock until slightly thickened.
Season with salt and white pepper.
Slowly drizzle beaten eggs in a thin stream while stirring the soup gently.
Turn off heat and finish with sesame oil and chopped scallions.

recipes.title: Beef Chow Fun
recipes.description: Cantonese stir-fried wide rice noodles with beef, bean sprouts and scallions in dark soy.
steps: Slice beef thinly against the grain and marinate with cornstarch, wine and 1 tablespoon soy sauce.
Gently separate the noodles by hand.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a wok and sear beef in a single layer, then remove.
Add remaining oil and noodles, stir-fry briefly to char and warm through.
Drizzle dark soy sauce, remaining soy sauce and sugar; toss until evenly coloured.
Add bean sprouts, scallions, chives and beef and toss for 1 minute.
